speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: SAS and OKC will face off in the Western Conference Finals
argument: Spurs' playoff readiness: Wembanyama, Castle, and Fox look like seasoned playoff players despite the Spurs' youth; Spurs' chemistry: San Antonio does not care who scores and lets whoever has it going lead, which Draymond calls their secret sauce; Thunder's injury concern: OKC can survive the next round without Jalen Williams, but Draymond thinks a Spurs matchup becomes much tougher if Williams is still out.
grade_note: SAS and OKC did face off in the Western Conference Finals, with SAS winning 4-3.
grade_note_general: SAS and OKC did face off in the Western Conference Finals, with SAS winning 4-3.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: MIN will not close out DEN in Game 6
argument: Missing star offense: Anthony Edwards being out leaves Minnesota without enough offense and without anyone to pressure Jokic on the other end; Defensive drop-off: when Minnesota cannot score, its belief, body language, and connected defense fall off, which also weakens Rudy Gobert's impact; Jokic matchup outlook: Draymond says another dominant Jokic game would not surprise him because Minnesota's offense is making its defense easier to break down.
grade_note: MIN won Game 6 on 2026-04-30 (110-98), closing out DEN 4-2; MIN did close out DEN in Game 6, contradicting the prediction.
